question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Maryland and Massachusetts have similar GDPs.
options.B: The GDP differences between the highlighted states are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.C: The GDP gap between Maryland and Massachusetts might exceed that between Ohio and Florida.
options.D: Ohio and Florida have nearly identical GDP levels.

explanation: Maryland (160.0$B) and Massachusetts (230.6$B) have a 70.6$B GDP difference, while Ohio (79.8$B) and Florida (80.4$B) have only a 0.6$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Maryland and Massachusetts into the same color bin (150-300$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while Ohio and Florida appear more different due to different color bins.
